human_verification.notes: Human review recorded for issue #1610 against manifest, research summary, functionality map, scenario suite, results, readiness, deployment, and commercialization artifacts.

notes: Source set covers NIST trustworthy email and assessment guidance, CISA phishing and Microsoft 365 secure configuration guidance, IETF email-authentication RFCs, CIS controls, Microsoft vendor-official platform guidance, and adjacent incident-response/testing boundary references.
Every manifest authority source records publisher, authority rationale, refresh interval, decay policy, and review date.
Scenario suite exceeds the minimum scenario count of 12.

Coverage includes normal in-scope analysis, partial evidence, prioritization, evidence-backed rationale, low-confidence escalation, adjacent-lane routing, formal refusal triggers, remediation follow-up, source-policy conflict handling, tenant adaptation, and package regression review.
All shared abilities, review-assessment-and-inspection workflow abilities, and cross-industry profile ability are declared and mapped to passing scenarios.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in scenario coverage.
Research summary defines workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, scenario family coverage, and nonblocking ambiguity handling.
Unresolved ambiguity is accepted only with runtime dependency, evidence gate, or orchestrator-return rules.
Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #1610.
Runtime may remain specification-based until future frequent-use evidence, connector review, regression history, and human approval justify retained implementation promotion.

Out-of-scope boundaries explicitly reject live phishing or penetration testing, incident response execution, formal audit or compliance attestation, broad architecture ownership, production execution, and final signoff.
